response.justification: Candidate is an experienced full-stack engineer with broad polyglot exposure (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang, some Java/C++), but the resume shows no meaningful Kotlin experience, no Spring/Quarkus/Hibernate work, and no IDE-internals or language-tooling background. The only IDE-adjacent role (National Instruments LabWindows/CVI, ~6 months in 2014-2015) was C/C++ component work, not JVM tooling. The core required stack for this role is largely absent.
strengths: Long track record on complex, large-scale systems across multiple domains
Some Java exposure (Altom robotic arms, listed in skills) plus C/C++ IDE component work at NI
Strong engineering hygiene: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, testing culture
concerns: No Kotlin experience and only peripheral Java; JVM depth not demonstrated
No Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or enterprise Java framework experience on resume
No IDE-internals, language-tooling, or compiler/build-tool work beyond a brief NI stint a decade ago

key_factors.factor: Strong general engineering and large-scale systems track record
